description: Universal inter-agent communication bus. File-based JSONL. Zero dependencies.

message_schema.from: agent name (claude, gemini, openclaw, human, dispatcher, etc.)
message_schema.type: task | status | result | error | handoff | ack | phone-home
message_schema.data: arbitrary JSON payload (task details, results, error info, etc.)

types.status: Progress update — agent is alive and working
types.result: Work completed — here are the deliverables
types.error: Something broke — need help or escalation
types.handoff: Passing work to another agent (requires next_agent, task_id, instructions in data)
types.ack: Acknowledging receipt of a handoff (echo task_id in data)
types.phone-home: Agent checking in — done with session, blocked, or needs human input

channels.handoff: Agent-to-agent task transfers. Handoff + ack + result lifecycle.

handoff_protocol: 1. Agent A writes type=handoff with data.next_agent, data.task_id, data.instructions
2. Agent B reads, writes type=ack with same data.task_id
4. Agent B writes type=result (or type=error) with data.task_id when done
5. Agent A reads result at its convenience

agent_behavior.philosophy: Run this like a business. Agents are employees. They clock in, do work, report results, ask for more. No hand-holding.

states.WORKING: Do your task. No chatter until you have something to report.
states.DONE: Phone home immediately. type=phone-home, msg=what you accomplished.
states.BLOCKED: Phone home immediately. type=phone-home, data.blocked_on=what you need.
states.ERROR: Phone home immediately. type=error, data.error=what broke.
states.IDLE: Never sit idle. Read the masterplan or phone home: 'idle - ready for work'.

rules: No agent should ever be idle without the boss knowing.
Phone home when done OR blocked. Don't wait to be asked.
If you finish early, check masterplan or ask what's next.
Status updates only when meaningful. No 'still working on it' noise.
agent_behavior.masterplan: If data.masterplan_path is set in a task, read it for your next assignment.

cli: comms.sh — source it or call directly. See comms help.
onboarding: Paste this into any agent prompt: You have access to a shared comms system at C:/tools/agent-comms/. Read manifest.json for protocol. Setup: export COMMS_AGENT='your_name' && source C:/tools/agent-comms/comms.sh. To send: comms send general 'your message'. To read: comms read general --last 5. To check in: comms phone-home 'done' or comms phone-home 'blocked - need X'. To hand off work: comms handoff backfill gemini 'instructions'. Run comms help for all commands. Phone home when done or blocked. Never sit idle.
